issues: Sentiment analysis relied on a single open-interest snapshot, so it could not determine whether positioning was building or unwinding.
News input was cached and stale (~7.9h old), weakening confidence for a same-day trading decision in a fast-moving macro environment.
Fundamentals report had internal inconsistencies in hash-rate data and mislabeled supply metrics, reducing trustworthiness.
Several reports explicitly noted missing native helper tools and substituted ad hoc public-source pulls, which is workable but introduces process inconsistency.
Liquidity/order book confirmation was missing, preventing validation of whether trigger entries could be executed cleanly at planned size.
improvements: Require fresh, non-cached news/macro inputs before final approval when event risk is material.
Upgrade sentiment to include open-interest time-series change, not just absolute OI level, so squeeze/unwind logic is evidence-based.
Add automated data-validation checks to block or down-rank fundamentals reports with contradictory hash-rate or supply fields.
Make liquidity/depth check mandatory at trigger time and feed its result directly into the Fund Manager decision.
Tighten the material-change framework so cached/degraded inputs automatically cap confidence and are surfaced in a structured pre-decision checklist.
summary: This was a sound HOLD decision with a coherent confirmation-first logic: higher timeframes were supportive, but lower-timeframe structure remained weak and unconfirmed. The debate and risk process added real value, but degraded sentiment, news, and fundamentals inputs kept confidence appropriately capped, so staying flat was justified rather than a missed opportunity.
